diagnosis.final_diagnosis: pheochromocytoma with coexisting non-ACTH-dependent cortisol hypersecretion
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with mild Cushing’s stigmata, hypertension, and diabetes mellitus. Hormonal work-up revealed elevated urinary catecholamines and dexamethasone non-suppressible hypercortisolism with low ACTH levels, suggesting non-ACTH-dependent cortisol hypersecretion. MRI findings and positive immunostaining for IL-6 in the pheochromocytoma suggested paracrine stimulation of the ipsilateral adrenal cortex by IL-6, leading to cortical hyperplasia and subclinical Cushing’s syndrome.
